prompts.text: Is it better to have a small group of close friends or a wide circle of acquaintances?

Do you actually know yourself, or do other people know you better?
What's the strongest case for never owning a car?
Make the best argument for going to bed at 9pm every night.
Argue the strongest case for deleting all your social media.
Make the case for never lending money to friends.
What's the best argument for skipping college entirely?

Make the strongest case that having children is selfish, then argue the other way.

Make the best case for telling white lies regularly.

Make the strongest case for staying in a job you dislike.

Tell me about the best meal you've ever had — and why it matters now.
Describe a habit you broke — and why anyone should care.
Tell me about your morning routine — and why it actually matters.
Describe your closest friendship — and what it proves about friendship in general.
Tell me about a place you love — and why it changes how you see other places.
Describe a compliment you remember — and what it reveals.
Tell me about the last argument you had — and why the topic actually mattered.
Describe a piece of advice you ignored — and what it cost you.
Walk me through how you choose what to eat for dinner — and why that process says something about you.
Tell me about a person who shaped you — and what difference it makes today.
Describe a recent purchase — and what it reveals about your values.
Tell me about a time you changed your mind — and why that change still matters.
Describe how you spent last Saturday — and what that says about what you actually value.
Tell me about a fear you have — and what difference it makes in how you live.
Describe a tradition in your family — and what it actually does for the people in it.
Tell me about a movie or book that stuck with you — and what it changed.
Describe the last time you cried — and what the world should learn from it.
Tell me what makes you angry — and why anyone else should care.
Describe what you think about right before falling asleep — and what it reveals.
Tell me about a regret you carry — and what it should teach a stranger.
Argue why your last boss was right about something you disagreed with.
Argue from the perspective of someone who loves the food you hate most.
Argue as someone who is glad it rained on their wedding day.
Argue from the perspective of a neighbor who thinks your music is too loud.
Argue as your younger self about a choice your current self made.
Argue from the perspective of someone who thinks small talk is the best part of the day.
Argue from the perspective of the parent who set the rule you most resented as a kid.
Argue as a person who has never owned a phone and is happier for it.
Argue from the perspective of someone who genuinely prefers winter to summer.
Argue as the friend who ghosted you — make their reasoning sound human.
Argue from the perspective of someone who finds your hobby ridiculous.
Argue as a person who chose not to have children and has no regrets.
Argue from the perspective of an ex defending why the relationship had to end.
Argue as someone who quit a job everyone else thought was perfect.
Argue from the perspective of someone older than you defending a value you reject.
Argue as a sibling who feels you got better treatment growing up.
Argue from the perspective of your future self at 80, defending a regret.
Argue as the person you cut out of your life — make their case.
Argue from the perspective of someone who finds your strongest belief naive.
Argue as a stranger watching you live your life — what would they say you're missing?
